member.name: Luke Taylor

addresses: Ground Floor, 3 Villiers Court, 40 Upper Mulgrave Road, Cheam, Sutton, SM2 7AJ

member.synopsis: Luke Taylor is the Liberal Democrat MP for Sutton and Cheam , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (London).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Developing Countries: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to help ensure that access to safe and good quality education for children overseas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government is committed to promoting equal access to safe, quality and inclusive education for all children worldwide, with a particular focus on girls, children with disabilities, and those displaced due to conflict and the climate crisis. We work...

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurological Diseases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to reduce avoidable emergency admissions for people with neurological conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to reducing avoidable emergency admissions for people with neurological conditions by improving earlier diagnosis, strengthening community-based care, and ensuring better coordination between services.NHS England is encouraging...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Origin Marking: Israel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent discussions he has had with Cabinet colleagues about imports from Israeli settlements mislabeled as originating from Israel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have had several discussions with colleagues on trade with and within the Israeli settlements in Palestine, which are illegal under international law. We strongly advise against conducting any economic and financial activities in settlements.Goods produ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Emergency Services: Health
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to increase support for emergency service personnel whose mental or physical health is affected in the line of duty.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through the measures set out in the Government’s Police Reform White Paper, “From Local to National: A New Model for Policing” we are taking steps to strengthen wellbeing provision for the policing workforce, including enhanced trauma support and occupati...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sangeethsan Ganeskumar
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what discussions she has had with her Sri Lankan counterpart on the arrest of Sangeethsan Ganeskumar, also known as Hip Hop Sangee.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Officials at our High Commission in Colombo are monitoring this case, and the British High Commissioner raised it with the Sri Lankan authorities on 4 June.The UK remains firmly committed to protecting human rights in Sri Lanka, including securing truth, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment the Government has made of the potential impact of Loan Charge and disguised remuneration cases on affected individuals’ financial wellbeing and mental health.

writtenQuestions.heading: Community Infrastructure Levy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his Department compensate councils for money lost from previously approved planning proposals for Community Infrastructure Levy funding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government does not compensate local authorities for money lost from previously approved planning proposals for Community Infrastructure Levy (CIL) funding. CIL charging authorities are responsible for charging, collecting, spending and generally admi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Sutton
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to help ensure the Specialist Emergency Care Hospital in Belmont is delivered as quickly as possible under the New Hospital Programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have put the New Hospital Programme (NHP) on a sustainable footing, with a credible plan, timeline, and budget aligned with the Government’s fiscal rules. As set out in the NHP Plan for Implementation published in January 2025, main construction on the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Housing Allowance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Local Housing Allowance on levels of poverty among older private renters.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Work and Pensions reviewed Local Housing Allowance (LHA) at Autumn Budget and announced that rates would be maintained at their current levels for 2026/27. A range of factors, such as rent levels and poverty impacts, were consid...

writtenQuestions.heading: EU Countries: Immigration Controls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps the Government is taking to secure e-gate access for UK travellers at Schengen area border points.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Access to e-gates in the EU and Schengen area is not based on bilateral agreements but is granted at the unilateral discretion of Member States. We continue to work closely with individual Member States to press for expanded access to e-gates for British ...

writtenQuestions.heading: EU Countries: Immigration Controls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what estimate she has made of the number of EU member states permitting UK citizens to use e-gates at border crossings since the UK-EU Summit of 19 May 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Access to e-gates in the EU and Schengen area is not based on bilateral agreements but is granted at the unilateral discretion of Member States. We continue to work closely with individual Member States to press for expanded access to e-gates for British ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Cables and Telecommunications Cables: Seas and Oceans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking with European allies to protect underwater sea cables from Russian interference.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are working closely with our European allies to respond to threats against our collective critical national infrastructure. The Ministry of Defence has launched the Atlantic Bastion programme which will create an advanced hybrid naval force. It has als...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: UK Relations with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what discussions she has had with the Minister for the Cabinet Office on mutual recognition agreements for a) the certification of aircraft parts and maintenance approvals, b) maintenance engineer licences and
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers and officials in the Department for Transport maintain regular engagement with colleagues in the Cabinet Office on a range of matters related to the UK’s international aviation agreements and regulatory cooperation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: UK Relations with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether a) the certification of aircraft parts and maintenance approvals and b) maintenance engineer licences are a priority outcome for the UK ahead of the upcoming UK-EU summit.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has reset our relations with European partners in order to improve our economic and security cooperation following Brexit. We are committed to a constructive, and forward-looking relationship with the EU on aviation safety, including seeki...

writtenQuestions.heading: Peter Thiel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Prime Minister met Peter Thiel in (a) July and (b) August 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer given by my Rt Hon. Friend the Minister for the Cabinet Office during the General Debate on the Government’s response to the House’s Humble Address of 4 February 2026, on 3 June 2026, Official Report, Columns 1209-1210...
